projects.noResults: No projects found matching your criteria.

items.title: Fluxion - GPU PDE Solver
items.description: GPU-first PDE solver in Rust with zero-allocation hot path and automatic backend selection. Supports CUDA, ROCm, wgpu and CPU backends with advanced implicit/explicit solvers.
items.longDescription: Fluxion is a high-performance PDE solver written in Rust, designed for GPU-first computation. It features a zero-allocation hot path with enum dispatch for maximum performance. The solver supports multiple backends (CUDA, ROCm, wgpu via WGSL compute shaders, and CPU via faer+rayon) with automatic fallback chain selection. It includes explicit time-stepping methods (Forward Euler, RK2/RK4, SSP), implicit solvers (Crank-Nicolson, BDF, IMEX), Krylov linear solvers (CG, BiCGSTAB, GMRES) and multigrid preconditioning. GPU kernels handle stencil operations, reductions with Kahan summation, and fused compute operations.

items.title: Modular CI/CD Production Chain
items.description: New modular software production chain for industrial environment. Python architecture enabling parallel compilation and incremental production.
items.longDescription: During my apprenticeship at Thales, I designed and implemented a modular CI/CD production chain replacing the legacy solution. This new Python architecture enables build parallelization, optimized dependency management per OS/architecture, and improves autonomy from continuous integration tools. The system handles large-scale projects with complete traceability and multi-platform support. Results: significant improvement in compilation times, with gains ranging from 20% to 80% depending on execution mode.

items.title: RiemannOpt - Riemannian Optimization Library
items.description: High-performance Rust library for optimization on Riemannian manifolds. Implementation of geodesic gradient descent and retraction algorithms for machine learning.
items.longDescription: RiemannOpt is an optimization library developed in Rust, specialized in optimization methods on Riemannian manifolds. It implements state-of-the-art algorithms like Riemannian gradient descent, retraction methods, and parallel transport. The library offers exceptional performance thanks to Rust and finds applications in machine learning, computer vision, and signal processing. It includes optimizations for Grassmann and Stiefel manifolds, and SPD matrices.

items.title: MinePP - Minecraft Clone in C++
items.description: Voxel game engine developed from scratch in modern C++ with OpenGL. Procedural terrain generation, optimized chunk system, and custom physics engine.
items.longDescription: MinePP is a Minecraft clone developed entirely in modern C++ (C++20), using OpenGL for 3D rendering. The project implements a chunk system with frustum culling, procedural terrain generation via Perlin noise, custom physics engine with AABB collision detection, and dynamic lighting system. The architecture is designed to be extensible with an ECS (Entity Component System) and supports multithreading for terrain generation.

items.title: Wazuh AI Assistant - Cybersecurity API
items.description: On-premise REST API integrating an LLM to synthesize Wazuh reports. Containerized solution meeting C3 security constraints with multi-model support.
items.longDescription: Developed during my internship in Romania at Thales, this project consists of a secure REST API enabling automatic analysis and synthesis of Wazuh security reports via a locally hosted LLM. The solution, fully containerized with Docker, supports multiple models (TinyLlama, Qwen, Mistral 7B) and integrates Redis cache for performance optimization. The architecture includes PostgreSQL for account management, Qdrant for vector search in CVEs, and Nginx for TLS termination. Compliant with C3 security requirements.

items.title: Synaplex - TDA Engine for Neural Networks
items.description: High-performance Topological Data Analysis engine for neural networks, written in Rust with Python bindings. Optimized algorithms for persistent homology computation and neural network analysis.
items.longDescription: Synaplex is a high-performance Topological Data Analysis (TDA) engine designed for analyzing neural networks. Written in Rust with Python bindings via PyO3, it provides optimized algorithms for persistent homology computation via cohomology, Vietoris-Rips simplicial complex construction, and neural network topology analysis (activation spaces, weight matrices, loss landscapes). The system emphasizes efficiency through combinatorial encoding, parallel processing with Rayon, and optimized algorithms that avoid unnecessary complexity.

items.title: Personal Portfolio
items.description: Modern portfolio website developed with Vue 3 and TypeScript. Responsive design, smooth animations, and multilingual support.
items.longDescription: Personal portfolio developed with the latest web technologies. Uses Vue 3 with Composition API, TypeScript for strong typing, and Pinia for state management. The design is fully responsive with Tailwind CSS, includes custom animations and multilingual support (i18n) for 6 languages. Optimized for performance with lazy loading and code splitting.

items.title: Introduction to Deep Learning - Mathematical Foundations
items.description: Comprehensive 168-page applied mathematics course covering deep learning from the single neuron to Transformers. Rigorous mathematical derivations, complete algorithms and numerical examples.
items.longDescription: A complete applied mathematics course building a progressive mathematical understanding of deep learning. Starting from a single neuron performing binary classification, it generalizes to arbitrarily sized networks and advanced architectures. The 17 chapters cover: neuron foundations and sigmoid activation, probabilistic cost functions, gradient descent and backpropagation, vectorization, multi-layer networks, multi-class classification with softmax, activation functions, parameter initialization (Glorot/He), batch normalization, advanced optimizers (SGD momentum, Adam, AdamW), regularization (L1/L2, dropout, early stopping, data augmentation), convolutional neural networks (CNNs), recurrent networks (RNN, LSTM, GRU), and attention mechanisms with Transformers.

papers.title: Early Detection of Overfitting via Persistent Homology Analysis of Deep Neural Network Activations
papers.abstract: Overfitting constitutes a fundamental challenge in deep learning. Classical detection methods rely on monitoring validation loss, often a lagging indicator. This work proposes an approach based on Topological Data Analysis (TDA) to detect early signs of overfitting by computing the persistent homology of internal activations of neural networks. The study covers three datasets (Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10, MNIST), three architectures (shallow CNN, deep CNN, MLP), and four training set sizes, with five independent runs per configuration. TDA-based detectors achieve a 100% detection rate on all 8 configurations, triggering at epochs 18-28 on CNN architectures — 30 to 55 epochs before early stopping. A reproducible three-phase pattern (compression, stabilization, fragmentation) is identified in topological invariants across all tested CNN configurations.

papers.title: Introduction to Deep Learning: From Mathematical Foundations to Neural Networks
papers.abstract: A comprehensive applied mathematics course building a progressive mathematical understanding of deep learning. Starting from a single neuron performing binary classification, the course generalizes to arbitrarily sized networks before tackling advanced architectures (convolutions, RNNs, Transformers). Deep learning rests on three fundamental pillars: modeling (building a parametric function), optimization (finding the best parameters via gradient descent), and generalization (ensuring the model works on new data). The 17 chapters provide rigorous mathematical derivations, complete algorithms, and numerical examples covering cost functions, backpropagation, vectorization, batch normalization, advanced optimizers (Adam, AdamW), regularization techniques, CNNs, LSTMs, GRUs, attention mechanisms, and the full Transformer architecture.
